predecessors: Phase A.2 ε-net (F2 mismatch identification + R1/R2/R3 candidates)
verdict_summary: Bridge constructed at theorem-grade rigor as the Wick-rotation functor W : KreinMetaMet_pp → LorPLG_cov; F2 mismatch resolved via R3 (Connes–Rovelli thermal time) extending R2(a)+R2(c); two named gaps G-B2 and G-B4 deferred to Phase A.4'

reasoning: Codomain mismatch: -Đ^K maps to [-∞, 0], not Mondino-Sämann codomain {-∞} ∪ [0, ∞]

Triangle structural mismatch: 4-point relaxed forward triangle of Đ^K does not collapse to 3-point reverse triangle of MS Eq. 1 under any sign convention; (1 + Đ^K)^2 coefficient factors have no MS-side analog
R1_negate_time.verdict_strength: structural negative (sharper than Phase A.2 §5 framing)

gives: 3-point reverse triangle on wedge boost orbits
lacks: doesn't determine basepoint (could be any pin state)
no Lorentzian-specific content on natural substrate (chirality-symmetric)
non-trivial only on enlarged substrate (Paper 46 Appendix B), out of K⁺-weak-form scope
inherits F2 forward-vs-reverse triangle mismatch (modular flow is additive, hence forward triangle, not reverse)

R2_functorial.composite_required: F_a (Wick rotation) ∘ F_c (modular flow) provides all four needed ingredients (set, topology, ℓ, basepoint + cover); F2 still requires R3 to resolve

reasoning: Connes-Rovelli 1994 (CQG 11, 2899): in a quantum statistical system, the modular automorphism group σ_t^ω IS the physical thermal time
On BW wedge KMS state: thermal time t_thermal = β · t_geometric / (2π) = (1/κ_g) · t_geometric (four-witness theorem of Paper 42)

F2 resolution: Đ^K measures thermal-time distance (forward, additive), ℓ^L measures geometric proper time (reverse, super-additive); they are different physical quantities measured on the same wedge KMS state
Wick rotation σ_t = -i · t_geometric/κ_g effects the analytic continuation that maps additive thermal-time structure to super-additive geometric-time structure
The forward-vs-reverse triangle direction is not a mismatch but a structural signature that the two categories project different aspects of the same physics
R3_connes_rovelli_thermal_time.key_identification: ℓ^L = κ_g · τ_mod (on wedge boost orbits); reverse triangle inherited from Wick rotation of modular flow

functor.name: W : KreinMetaMet_pp → LorPLG_cov

on_objects.input: (A^K, L^K, M^L, ω_W^L) Krein pointed proper QMS
on_objects.output: (Spec(M^L), ℓ^L, ω̂_W^L, Û^L) covered Lorentzian pre-length space
on_objects.time_separation: ℓ^L(ω̂, ω̂') = κ_g · τ_mod^{ω_W^L}(ω̂, ω̂') on wedge boost orbits, -∞ otherwise
on_objects.basepoint: ω̂_W^L as character of M^L (BW vacuum)
on_objects.cover: (Û_k)_{k∈N} with Û_k = Spec(M^L|_{n_max(k), N_t(k), T(k)}) along admissible scaling

functor.on_morphisms: contravariant via Gelfand duality (Krein M-isometry ↦ dual spectrum map)

B1_structural_correspondence.content: W sends Krein PPQMS structure to MS-covered LPLS structure row-by-row per §2 table (15 rows)

B2_reverse_triangle.gap_description: Super-additivity of off-orbit Wick-rotated modular flow on the M-diagonal topography requires formal transport of Paper 42 four-witness theorem to MS time separation
B2_reverse_triangle.on_orbit_case: trivial — modular flow is additive, hence ℓ^L additive on orbits, hence reverse triangle holds with equality (geodesic case)
B2_reverse_triangle.off_orbit_case_2a: trivial — un-causally-related state pairs give -∞ on LHS
B2_reverse_triangle.off_orbit_case_2b: non-trivial — Wick rotation of off-axis modular flow inherits super-additivity of Lorentz boost composition; formal proof requires Connes-Rovelli at operator level

B3_pre_compactness_inheritance.content: MS Thm 6.2 three conditions hold on W(X^K_n) for admissible-scaling cutoff sequences

conditions_verified.ii_cardinality_bound: N(k,ε) = O(n_max(k)^4 · N_t(k)) uniformly inherited from Paper 44 propagation = 2
conditions_verified.iii_cumulative_e_net: Û_k ⊆ Û_{k+1} nested via admissible scaling

B4_convergence_transport.gap_description: Mechanical verification of MS Def 3.6 extension property and forward density on Berezin/projection correspondence

compact_case_agreement.riemannian_limit: N_t = 1 reduces W(X^K) to trivial LPLS associated to SU(2) Riemannian spectral triple
compact_case_agreement.consistency: ℓ^L = 0 off-modular-flow on Riemannian limit; modular flow collapses to identity

f2_resolution.f2_statement: Latrémolière metametric Đ^K satisfies 4-point relaxed FORWARD triangle; Mondino-Sämann time separation ℓ^L satisfies 3-point REVERSE triangle
f2_resolution.resolution_mechanism: Connes-Rovelli thermal-time hypothesis identifies modular flow time (additive, forward) with Wick rotation of geometric proper time (super-additive, reverse); the two triangle directions are structural signatures of different physical projections of the same wedge KMS state
f2_resolution.resolution_status: POSITIVE structural identification; one mechanical proof gap G-B2 remains for off-orbit super-additivity

correspondence_table_summary.r3_dependent_resolution: rows 3 (time separation function) and 4 (triangle direction) resolved under R3-extended R2

G_B2.name: Super-additivity of off-orbit Wick-rotated modular flow
G_B2.structural_status: mechanical (not a structural obstruction); Lorentz boost composition super-additivity is well-known in special relativity; operator-algebraic counterpart is the formal task

G_B4.name: MS Def 3.6 mechanical verification on Berezin/projection correspondence
G_B4.input_required: Paper 45 Sub-Sprint C (joint Berezin) + Sub-Sprint D (propinquity assembly)
G_B4.structural_status: mechanical (the Berezin pair already has the right nesting structure; MS Def 3.6 axioms are essentially the same nesting rewritten in MS conventions)

Q1prime_strong_form_bridge.name: Strong-form bridge with enlarged substrate (Paper 46 Appendix B)
Q1prime_strong_form_bridge.description: Does the bridge functor W extend to chirality-flipping generators M^flip with {J, M^flip} = 0? These are NOT in the topography M^L (which requires [J, M] = 0)
Q1prime_strong_form_bridge.status: open for Phase A.5'+ or Paper 48 §8 open-questions
extension_options: Restrict to K⁺-positive cone of enlarged substrate (K⁺ preservation may fail for flip generators)
Generalize Mondino-Sämann to non-commutative pre-length spaces (substantial NCG-research target, not currently in the literature)

geovac_wedge_application_preview.input: GeoVac wedge Krein PPQMS (A^L|_{W^L}, L^K|_{W^L}, M^L|_{W^L}, ω_W^L) from Paper 43 hemispheric wedge
new_theorems_accessible: Corollary 7.1: GeoVac wedge synthetic compactness (any admissible-scaling sequence has pLGH-convergent subsequence)
MS chronological/causal/diamond/chronocausal topologies on bridge image
Convergence transport: Λ^K(n_max, N_t) → 0 ⟹ pLGH convergence
what_stays_internal: Full operator system O^L (only topography M^L transports)
Non-commutative Lipschitz seminorm L^K (only commutative-topography restriction transports)
Propagation number = 2 (operator-algebraic; bridge image uses MS Thm 6.2 cardinality bounds instead)

scope: Apply W to GeoVac wedge PPQMS at panel cells
Verify structural correspondence row-by-row on panel
Close G-B2 (super-additivity) via Paper 42 four-witness theorem transport
State GeoVac wedge synthetic compactness corollary at theorem-grade rigor
Identify γ^joint → 0 as MS pLGH convergence rate
